export-fields.description: Export fields parameter, used to change default order or ignore some of the response properties. Default is empty array, which means that all fields of the response entity will be serialized. Example: export-fields=id,username
export-fields.name: export-fields

export-labels.description: Export labels parameter, used to customize fieldnames/columns in the exported file. Default is empty array, which means that response properties names will be used. Number of the provided labels must match the number of export-fields Example: export-labels=identifier,name with matching: export-fields=id,username
export-labels.name: export-labels

filter.description: Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter results. Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page. Fields allowed in the query: status, updated, version This param can be combined with paging and sorting. Example: filter=username!=admin and details==*disabled* and date<2019-12-15
filter.name: filter

sort.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort order is descending. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be entered on separate lines in Swagger UI. In the URI the 'sort' query param is not duplicated for each sort criterion, e.g., ...&sort=name:asc,date:desc. Fields that can be sorted: status, updated
sort.name: sort

code.description: Error-specific code that can be used to identify localization string, etc.

description.description: A general description of error for troubleshooting/debugging. Generally this text should not be displayed to a user; instead refer to errorCode and it's localized text
description.example: I've just picked up a fault in the AE35 unit. It's going to go 100% failure in 72 hours

field.description: Name of the field that caused the error.

id.description: id of object with error. Optional.

selected.description: If true the device should be added to the group, if false should be removed from the group

templateCategoryName.description: Template category name of the extension attribute.

templateId.description: Unique Id for Computer Extension Attribute Template.

templateName.description: Template display name of the extension attribute.

enabled.description:
Enabled by default, but for inputType Script we can disable it as well.
Possible values are:
false
true

inputType.description: Extension attributes collect inventory data by using an input type.The type of the Input used to populate the extension attribute.

inventoryDisplayType.description: Category in which to display the extension attribute in Jamf Pro.

ldapAttributeMapping.description:
Directory Service attribute use to populate the extension attribute.
Required when inputType is "DIRECTORY_SERVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPING"

ldapExtensionAttributeAllowed.description:
Collect multiple values for this extension attribute. ldapExtensionAttributeAllowed is disabled by default, only for inputType 'DIRECTORY_SERVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPING' it can be enabled. It's value cannot be modified during edit operation.
Possible values are:
false
true

manageExistingData.description: It is used to specify to either delete or retain the extension attributes values when inputType is Script and enabled is false.

popupMenuChoices.description: When added with list of choices while creating computer extension attributes these Pop-up menu can be displayed in inventory information. User can choose a value from the pop-up menu list when enrolling a computer any time using Jamf Pro. Provide popupMenuChoices only when inputType is 'POPUP'.

scriptContents.description: When we run this script it returns a data value each time a computer submits inventory to Jamf Pro. Provide scriptContents only when inputType is 'SCRIPT'.

description.example: A group containing all managed computers

hyperlink.description: Link to dependent object or to page with list of dependent objects.

nameLocalization.description: Name of localization which display dependent object.

objectId.description: Object Type Id of the dependency object.

DetailsV2.description: will be populated if the type is ios or visionos.

batteryHealth.description:
- NON_GENUINE: The battery isn’t a genuine Apple battery.
- NORMAL: The battery is operating normally.
- SERVICE_RECOMMENDED: The system recommends battery service.
- UNKNOWN: The system couldn’t determine battery health information.
- UNSUPPORTED: The device doesn’t support battery health reporting.

clearActivationLock.description: Clear the activation lock on the device.

disallowProximitySetup.description: If 'true', disable Proximity Setup on the next reboot and skip the pane in Setup Assistant.

preserveDataPlan.description: If 'true', preserve the data plan on an iPhone or iPad with eSIM functionality, if one exists.

returnToService.description: If 'true', the device will be returned to service after the erase is complete.

commandUuid.description: Uuid of the queued eraseDevice command

deviceId.description: Id of the mobile device for which eraseDevice command was queued

ExportField.description: Field to be included in the export operation.

fieldLabelOverride.description: Name which should be used for the label in the response - can be in any language. When null the fieldName itself will be used as the label.

fieldName.description: English name of the field to be exported.

fields.description: Used to change default order or ignore some of the fields. When null or empty array, all fields will be exported.

sort.description: Sorting criteria in the format: [<property>[:asc/desc]. Default direction when not stated is ascending.

id.description: Unique Id for Mobile Device Extension Attribute.

name.description: Display name for the extension attribute.

batteryHealth.description:
- NON_GENUINE: The battery isn’t a genuine Apple battery.
- NORMAL: The battery is operating normally.
- SERVICE_RECOMMENDED: The system recommends battery service.
- UNKNOWN: The system couldn’t determine battery health information.
- UNSUPPORTED: The device doesn’t support battery health reporting.

personalDeviceProfileCurrent.description: **Deprecated as of 11.25.** This field always returns false.

subjectName.example: CN=Fleet Docker Jamf Pro JSS Built-in Certificate Authority

enforceName.description: Enforce the mobile device name. Device must be supervised. If set to true, Jamf Pro will revert the Mobile Device Name to the ‘name’ value each time the device checks in.

osRapidSecurityResponse.description: Collected for iOS 16 and iPadOS 16.1 or later

osSupplementalBuildVersion.description: Collected for iOS 16 and iPadOS 16.1 or later

type.description: Based on the value of this either iOS, tvOS, watch or visionOS objects will be populated.

inputType.description: Extension attributes collect inventory data by using an input type.The type of the Input used to populate the extension attribute.

inventoryDisplayType.description: Category in which to display the extension attribute in Jamf Pro.

ldapAttributeMapping.description:
Directory Service attribute use to populate the extension attribute.
Required when inputType is "DIRECTORY_SERVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPING"

ldapExtensionAttributeAllowed.description:
Collect multiple values for this extension attribute. ldapExtensionAttributeAllowed is disabled by default, only for inputType 'DIRECTORY_SERVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPING' it can be enabled. It's value cannot be modified during edit operation.
Possible values are:
false
true

popupMenuChoices.description: When added with list of choices while creating mobile device extension attributes these Pop-up menu can be displayed in inventory information. User can choose a value from the pop-up menu list when enrolling a mobile device any time using Jamf Pro. Provide popupMenuChoices only when inputType is 'POPUP'.

deviceOwnershipType.description: The enrollment method used for the device. **Note:** The `PersonalDeviceProfile` enrollment method was removed as of 11.25.

description.example: A group containing all managed iPads

batteryHealth.description:
- NON_GENUINE: The battery isn’t a genuine Apple battery.
- NORMAL: The battery is operating normally.
- SERVICE_RECOMMENDED: The system recommends battery service.
- UNKNOWN: The system couldn’t determine battery health information.
- UNSUPPORTED: The device doesn’t support battery health reporting.

deviceType.description: Based on the value of this type either ios, appleTv, watch or visionOS objects will be populated.

MobileDeviceNetwork.description: This section only avaiable for Ios type.

eid.description: EID or "embedded identity document" is a number associated with the eSIM on a device

MobileDeviceSecurity.description: This section only available for Ios type.

bootstrapTokenEscrowed.description: Indicates the bootstrap token escrow status for the device

personalDeviceProfileCurrent.description: **Deprecated as of 11.25.** This field always returns false

labelId.description: The unique identifier for this subscription.

slot.description: The description of the slot that contains the SIM representing this subscription.

eid.description: EID or "embedded identity document" is a number associated with the eSIM on a device

details.example: Is SSO Enabled false\nSelected SSO Provider

note.example: A generic note can sometimes be useful, but generally not.

info.example: A package that is important to my organization.

hasNext.description: True if there are more pages after the current page

hasPrevious.description: True if there are pages before the current page

results.description: List of users in the current page

totalCount.description: Total number of users matching the search criteria

notes.example: Should be able to be re-run without problem.

bootstrapToken.description: The bootstrap token for the device

bootstrapTokenEscrowed.description: Indicates the bootstrap token escrow status for the device

groupDescription.example: A smart group containing iPads meeting certain criteria
Smart iPads that meet certain criteria

groupId.description: The unique identifier of the smart group

andOr.description: Whether this criterion should be ANDed or ORed with the previous criterion

closingParen.description: Whether to add a closing parenthesis after this criterion

name.description: The field to search on (e.g., Model, OS Version, etc.)

openingParen.description: Whether to add an opening parenthesis before this criterion

priority.description: The priority order of this criterion

searchType.description: The type of search to perform (e.g., is, is not, like, etc.)

criteria.description: The criteria used to define the smart group

SmartGroupMembership.description: the ids of the computers that are members of the smart group

warning.example: Converting buildings, do not restart your JSS

assignments.description: Array of computer IDs to assign to the static group

groupDescription.example: A static group containing only iPads

TvOsDetails.description: will be populated if the type is appleTv.

commandUuid.description: Uuid of the command queued that removes the MDM profile

deviceId.description: Id of the mobile device whose MDM profile was removed

enforceName.description: Enforce the mobile device name. Device must be supervised. If set to true, Jamf Pro will revert the Mobile Device Name to the ‘name’ value each time the device checks in.

name.description: Mobile Device Name. When updated, Jamf Pro sends an MDM settings command to the device (device must be supervised).

WatchOsDetailsV2.description: will be populated if the type is watchos.

ApiClient.description: Jamf Pro API Roles and Clients provide a client ID and secret for obtaining access tokens using the OAuth2 client_credentials grant type

BasicAuth.description: Basic auth is used only for obtaining bearer tokens from the /v1/auth/token endpoint

info.description:
## Overview The Jamf Pro API is a RESTful API for Jamf Pro built to enable consistent and efficient programmatic access to Jamf Pro.

The swagger schema can be found [here](/api/schema/).
info.title: Jamf Pro API

get.description: Retrieves information about application startup. Current startup operation taking place (if any) and overall startup completion percentage.
get.summary: Retrieve information about application startup

get.description: Gets Advanced Search Objects
get.summary: Get Advanced Search objects

post.description: Creates Advanced Search Object

201.description: Successful response - Advanced search created

get.description: Gets Mobile Device Advanced Search criteria choices. A list of potentially valid choices can be found by navigating to the Criteria page of the Advanced Mobile Device Search creation process. A few are "App Name", "Building", and "Display Name".

post.description: Removes specified Advanced Search Objects

requestBody.description: ids of the building to be deleted

get.description: Search for sorted and paged buildings

parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort is id:asc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with a comma. Example: sort=date:desc,name:asc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter buildings collection. Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page. Fields allowed in the query: name, streetAddress1, streetAddress2, city, stateProvince, zipPostalCode, country. This param can be combined with paging and sorting. Example: filter=city=="Chicago" and name=="*build*"
parameters.name: filter

get.summary: Search for sorted and paged Buildings

post.description: Create building record

requestBody.description: building object to create. ids defined in this body will be ignored

201.description: Building record was created

post.description: Export Buildings collection

parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort is id:desc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with a comma. Example: sort=id:desc,name:asc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter history notes collection. Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page. Fields allowed in the query: id, name. This param can be combined with paging and sorting. Example: name=="*buildings*"
parameters.name: filter

requestBody.description: Optional. Override query parameters since they can make URI exceed 2,000 character limit.

400.description: Export fields specified incorrectly - non-existing column or duplicate on the list.

503.description: Export currently unavailable, all export-related resources are in use. Try again later.

parameters.description: instance id of building record
parameters.name: id

get.description: Gets specified Building object

parameters.description: instance id of building record
parameters.name: id

404.description: Specified building object does not exist.

get.summary: Get specified Building object

parameters.description: instance id of building record
parameters.name: id

requestBody.description: building object to update. ids defined in this body will be ignored

get.description: Gets `Category` objects.

parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort is id:asc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with a comma. Example: sort=date:desc,name:asc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter categories collection. Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page. Fields allowed in the query: name, priority. This param can be combined with paging and sorting. Example: filter=name=="Apps*" and priority>=5
parameters.name: filter

get.summary: Get Category objects

requestBody.description: category object to create. IDs defined in this body will be ignored

requestBody.description: IDs of the categories to be deleted

204.description: All Category IDs passed in request sucessfully deleted.

400.description: Request processed.  Returns IDs and error codes of any categories unable to be deleted.  Expected error codes: INVALID_ID - id does not exist SYSTEM_EXCEPTION - a system exception occurred trying to delete the category.

parameters.description: instance id of category record
parameters.name: id

parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter history notes collection. Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page. Fields allowed in the query: username, date, note, details. This param can be combined with paging and sorting. Example: filter=username!=admin and details==*disabled* and date<2019-12-15
parameters.name: filter

200.description: Details of category history were found

404.description: Specified category object does not exist.

get.description: Retrieves All Computer Extension Attributes Configuration.

parameters.description:
Sorts results by one or more criteria, following the format property:asc/desc.
Default sort is name:asc.
If using multiple criteria, separate with commas. Allows sort for id and name.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
Filters results. Use RSQL format for query. Allows for many fields, including ID, name, etc.
Can be combined with paging and sorting.
Fields allowed in the query: id, name
Default filter is an empty query and returns all results from the requested page.
parameters.name: filter

200.description: Computer Extension Attribute objects has been fetched successfully.

404.description: Computer Extension Attribute objects could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

get.summary: Retrieve Computer Extension Attributes.

post.description: Create Computer Extension Attribute to collect extra inventory information.

requestBody.description: Computer Extension Attribute to be created.

201.description: Computer Extension Attribute was successfully created.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax. Possible reasons are:</br> 1) Required fields are not provided in request body.</br> 2) Fields which are not associated with provided inputType has some value.</br> 3) Any values outside of the allowed range are included in the request body.</br> 4) LDAP is not configured.</br> 5) Error occured during database create operation.</br> 6) Duplicate_Field

post.description: IDs of the Computer Extension Attribute to be deleted.

requestBody.description: IDs of the Computer Extension Attribute to be deleted

204.description: All specified Computer Extension Attribute deleted successfully.

400.description: Bad Request. Returns error codes and IDs for any Computer Extension Attribute that were unable to be deleted. Possible reasons are:</br> 1) The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.</br> 2) The specified extension attribute id does not exist in Jamf Pro.

post.summary: Delete multiple Computer Extension Attribute at once.

get.description: Retrieves All Computer Extension Attributes Templates.

parameters.description:
Sorts results by one or more criteria, following the format property:asc/desc.
Default sort is templateName:asc.
If using multiple criteria, separate with commas. Allows sort for templateName and templateCategory.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
Filters results. Use RSQL format for queries. which allows filtering by multiple fields such as templateName, templateCategoryName.
Can be combined with paging and sorting.
Fields allowed in the query: templateName, templateCategoryName
Default filter is an empty query and returns all results from the requested page.
parameters.name: filter

200.description: Computer Extension Attribute templates objects has been fetched successfully.

get.summary: Retrieve All Computer Extension Attributes Templates.

get.description: Gets specified Computer Extension Attribute Template object.

parameters.description: Unique Id of the Template.
parameters.name: id

200.description: Computer Extension Attribute Template object has been fetched successfully.

404.description: Specified Computer Extension Attribute Template object could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

get.summary: Get specified Computer Extension Attribute Template object.

post.description: Uploads a Computer Extension Attribute.

200.description: Computer Extension Attribute object has been fetched successfully.

404.description: Bad Request. Possible reasons are:</br> 1) Unsupported file extension. Acceptable format is xml.</br> 2) Uploaded file should not be empty.

delete.description: ID of the Computer Extension Attribute to be deleted.

parameters.description: Unique ID of Computer Extension Attribute.
parameters.name: id

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax. Possible reason can be 'The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.'

404.description: Specified Computer Extension Attribute object could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

get.description: Gets specified Computer Extension Attribute object.

parameters.description: Unique ID of Computer Extension Attribute.
parameters.name: id

200.description: Computer Extension Attribute object has been fetched successfully.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax. Possible reason can be 'The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.'

404.description: Specified Computer Extension Attribute object could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

get.summary: Get specified Computer Extension Attribute object.

put.description: Update specified Computer Extension Attribute object.

parameters.description: Unique ID of Computer Extension Attribute.
parameters.name: id

requestBody.description: Computer Extension Attribute object to be updated. IDs defined in this body will be ignored.

400.description: Bad Request. Specified Computer Extension Attribute could not be updated due to bad request. Possible reasons are:</br> 1) The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.</br> 2) Duplicate_Field.</br> 3) Required fields are not provided in request body.</br> 4) Fields which are not associated with provided inputType has some value.</br> 5) Any values outside of the allowed range are included in the request body.</br> 6) LDAP is not configured.</br> 7) Error occured during database update operation.

404.description: Specified Computer Extension Attribute object does not exist. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

put.summary: Update specified Computer Extension Attribute object.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax.

404.description: Specified computer extension attribute id were not found in database.

get.summary: Get smart group/advance search dependent objects for a specified computer extension attribute

200.description: The requested Computer Extension Attribute XML file was fetched successfully.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax. Possible reason can be 'The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.'

404.description: Specified Computer Extension Attribute object could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

get.summary: Download the specified Computer Extension Attribute.

parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Filters results. Use RSQL format for query. Allows for many fields, including ID, name, etc. Can be combined with paging and sorting. Default filter is an empty query and returns all results from the requested page.
parameters.name: filter

200.description: Details of Computer Extension Attribute history were found.

400.description: Bad Request. Specified Computer Extension Attribute history object could not be found. Possible reason can be 'Provided id field is not string of positive numeric value or -1'.

get.description: Returns group information for all Mobile Device and Computer groups. The type of groups returned will be dependent upon the corresponding group type READ privileges. Results can be sorted by name, description, group type, or isSmart. Default sorting is by group name in ascending order.

parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort is groupName:asc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with a comma. Fields allowed in sorting: groupName, groupDescription, groupType, isSmart. Example: sort=groupName:asc,groupType:desc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter group collection. Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page. Fields allowed in the query: groupName, groupDescription, groupType, isSmart. This param can be combined with paging and sorting. When using groupType in the filter, the value must be either "MOBILE" or "COMPUTER" but not both. When using groupType in the filter, the value is case sensitive. When using groupType in the filter, it will exclude groups of the other type regardless of or/and conditionals. Example: filter=groupName=="*Managed*" and isSmart=="true" Example: filter=groupType=="COMPUTER" and groupDescription=="*Admin*"
parameters.name: filter

get.summary: Returns group information for all Mobile Device and Computer groups

delete.description: Deletes a group by its platform UUID. Returns a 400 error if the group is being used as a dependency. Requires appropriate DELETE privileges.

parameters.description: The platform UUID of a group
parameters.name: id

400.description: Group cannot be deleted due to dependencies or validation error

403.description: Insufficient privileges to delete this group

404.description: A group with that platform UUID does not exist

delete.summary: Delete a group by platform UUID

get.description: Returns group information for the given platform UUID. Dependent upon the returned group type the corresponding READ privilege for that group type will be needed.

parameters.description: The platform UUID of a group
parameters.name: id

smartGroup.description: Example response for a smart group with criteria

staticGroup.description: Example response for a static group without criteria

value.groupDescription: All computers in the marketing department

404.description: A group with that platform UUID does not exist

get.summary: Returns group information for the given platform UUID

patch.description: Updates a group by its platform UUID. For both smart and static groups, groupName and groupDescription can be updated. For smart groups, criteria can also be updated. For static groups, assignments can also be updated. Requires appropriate UPDATE privileges.

parameters.description: The platform UUID of a group
parameters.name: id

smartGroup.description: Example of updating a smart group with criteria

staticGroup.description: Example of updating a static group with device assignments

400.description: Invalid request data or validation error

403.description: Insufficient privileges to update this group

get.description: Retrieves all mobile device extension attributes configuration.

parameters.description:
Sorts results by one or more criteria, following the format property:asc/desc.
Default sort is name:asc.
If using multiple criteria, separate with commas. Allows sort for id and name.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
Filters results. Use RSQL format for query. Allows for many fields, including ID, name, etc.
Can be combined with paging and sorting.
Fields allowed in the query: id, name
Default filter is an empty query and returns all results from the requested page.
parameters.name: filter

200.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute objects has been fetched successfully.

404.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute objects could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

get.summary: Retrieve Mobile Device Extension Attributes.

post.description: Create Mobile Device Extension Attribute to collect extra inventory information.

requestBody.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute to be created.

201.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute was successfully created.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax. Possible reasons are:</br> 1) Required fields are not provided in request body.</br> 2) Fields which are not associated with provided inputType has some value.</br> 3) Any values outside of the allowed range are included in the request body.</br> 4) LDAP is not configured.</br> 5) Error occurred during database create operation.</br> 6) Duplicate name is provided.</br>

delete.description:
Deletes the Mobile Device Extension Attribute identified by the provided ID.<\br>
In addition to removing the attribute itself, this operation will also delete any related dependent data, including:<\br>
  - Associated popup menu choices.<\br>
  - Fields used in saved search displays.<\br>
  - User preferences that reference the attribute.

parameters.description: Unique ID of Mobile Device Extension Attribute.
parameters.name: id

204.description: The Mobile Device Extension was successfully deleted.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not process the request due to invalid input. Possible reasons are:</br> - The specified extension attribute ID is not a valid number. - The ID is missing or improperly formatted.

404.description: Not Found. The specified Mobile Device Extension Attribute does not exist. This may indicate that the resource is missing, or the client is not authorized to access it.

delete.summary: Delete a Mobile Device Extension Attribute by ID.

get.description: Gets specified Mobile Device Extension Attribute object.

parameters.description: Unique ID of Mobile Device Extension Attribute.
parameters.name: id

200.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute object has been fetched successfully.

400.description: Bad Request. The server could not understand the request because of invalid syntax. Possible reason can be 'The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.'

404.description: Specified Mobile Device Extension Attribute object could not be found. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

parameters.name: id

requestBody.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute object to be updated. IDs defined in this body will be ignored.

202.description: Mobile Device Extension Attribute updated successfully.

400.description: Bad Request. Specified Mobile Device Extension Attribute could not be updated due to bad request. Possible reasons are:</br> 1) The specified extension attribute id does not include a valid number.</br> 2) Duplicate name is provided.</br> 3) Required fields are not provided in request body.</br> 4) Fields which are not associated with provided inputType has some value.</br> 5) Any values outside of the allowed range are included in the request body.</br> 6) LDAP is not configured.</br> 7) Error occurred during database update operation.

404.description: Specified Mobile Device Extension Attribute object does not exist. The server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort
is mobileDeviceId:asc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with
a comma. 

Fields allowed in the sort: `airPlayPassword`, `appAnalyticsEnabled`, `assetTag`, `availableSpaceMb`, 
`batteryLevel`, `batteryHealth`, `bluetoothLowEnergyCapable`, `bluetoothMacAddress`, `capacityMb`, 
`lostModeEnabledDate`, `declarativeDeviceManagementEnabled`, `deviceId`, `deviceLocatorServiceEnabled`,
`devicePhoneNumber`, `diagnosticAndUsageReportingEnabled`, `displayName`, `doNotDisturbEnabled`, 
`enrollmentSessionTokenValid`, `exchangeDeviceId`, `cloudBackupEnabled`, `osBuild`,
`osRapidSecurityResponse`, `osSupplementalBuildVersion`, `osVersion`, `ipAddress`, `itunesStoreAccountActive`,
`mobileDeviceId`, `managementId`, `languages`, `lastBackupDate`, `lastEnrolledDate`, `lastCloudBackupDate`,
`lastInventoryUpdateDate`, `locales`, `locationServicesForSelfServiceMobileEnabled`, `lostModeEnabled`,
`managed`, `mdmProfileExpirationDate`, `model`, `modelIdentifier`,
`modelNumber`, `modemFirmwareVersion`, `preferredVoiceNumber`, `quotaSize`, `residentUsers`,
`serialNumber`, `sharedIpad`, `supervised`, `tethered`,
`timeZone`, `udid`, `usedSpacePercentage`, `wifiMacAddress`,
`deviceOwnershipType`, `building`, `department`, `emailAddress`,
`fullName`, `userPhoneNumber`, `position`, `room`,
`username`, `appleCareId`, `leaseExpirationDate`,`lifeExpectancyYears`,
`poDate`, `poNumber`, `purchasePrice`, `purchasedOrLeased`,
`purchasingAccount`, `purchasingContact`, `vendor`, `warrantyExpirationDate`,
`activationLockEnabled`, `blockEncryptionCapable`, `dataProtection`, `fileEncryptionCapable`,
`hardwareEncryptionSupported`, `jailbreakStatus`, `passcodeCompliant`, `passcodeCompliantWithProfile`,
`passcodeLockGracePeriodEnforcedSeconds`, `passcodePresent`, `carrierSettingsVersion`,
`cellularTechnology`, `currentCarrierNetwork`, `currentMobileCountryCode`, `currentMobileNetworkCode`, 
`dataRoamingEnabled`, `eid`, `network`, `homeMobileCountryCode`, 
`homeMobileNetworkCode`, `iccid`, `imei`, `imei2`,
`meid`, `personalHotspotEnabled`, `voiceRoamingEnabled`, `roaming`, `lastLoggedInUsernameSelfService`, `lastLoggedInUsernameSelfServiceTimestamp`

Extension attributes can be sorted by using the format `EA+ID` where ID is the ID of the extension attribute, for example `EA+1!=null`

Example: `sort=displayName:desc,username:asc`
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter mobile device collection.
Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page.

Fields allowed in the query: `airPlayPassword`, `appAnalyticsEnabled`, `assetTag`, `availableSpaceMb`, 
`batteryLevel`, `bluetoothLowEnergyCapable`, `bluetoothMacAddress`, `capacityMb`, 
`declarativeDeviceManagementEnabled`, `deviceId`, `deviceLocatorServiceEnabled`, `devicePhoneNumber`,

`wifiMacAddress`, `building`, `department`, `emailAddress`,
`fullName`, `userPhoneNumber`, `position`, `room`,
`username`, `appleCareId`, `lifeExpectancyYears`, `poNumber`, 
`purchasePrice`, `purchasedOrLeased`, `purchasingAccount`, `purchasingContact`,
`vendor`, `activationLockEnabled`, `blockEncryptionCapable`, `dataProtection`, 
`fileEncryptionCapable`, `passcodeCompliant`, `passcodeCompliantWithProfile`, `passcodeLockGracePeriodEnforcedSeconds`,
`passcodePresent`, `carrierSettingsVersion`, `currentCarrierNetwork`,

get.description: Find site objects for Site ID, with the ability to filter out different object types and object IDs for the site ID

parameters.description: Site ID to get objects for
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: `property:asc/desc`. Default sort
is `objectType:asc`. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated
with a comma.

Example: `sort=objectId:asc,objectType:desc`.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
Query in the RSQL format, allowing filter of site object information.
Default filter returns all objects for the site ID.

Fields allowed in the query: `objectType`, `objectId`

Example: `filter=objectType=="User"`

List of `objectType` options (case-insensitive) ["Computer", "Peripheral", "Licensed Software", "Licensed Software Template", "Policy", "macOS Configuration Profile", "Restricted Software", "Managed Preference Profile", "Computer Group", "Mobile Device", "Apple TV", "Android Device", "User Group", "iOS Configuration Profile", "Mobile Device App", "E-book", "Mobile Device Group", "Classroom", "Advanced Computer Search", "Advanced Mobile Search", "Advanced User Search", "Advanced User Content Search", "Computer Invitation", "Mobile Device Invitation", "Mobile Device Enrollment Profile", "Device Enrollment Program Instance", "Mobile Device Prestage", "Computer DEP Prestage", "Enrollment Customization", "VPP Location", "VPP Subscription", "VPP Invitation", "VPP Assignment", "User", "Network Integration", "Mac App", "App Installer", "Self Service Plugin", "Software Title", "Patch Software Title Summary", "Patch Policy", "Patch Software Title Configuration", "Change Password", "Mobile Device Inventory", "Computer Inventory", "Change Management", "Licensed Software License"]
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Page to display. Uses zero-based indexing.

parameters.description: Number of results per page. Maximum of 1000.
parameters.name: page-size

parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: property(:asc|desc). Default sort order is ascending.
Multiple sort criteria are supported.

Examples:
- sort=username
- sort=username:asc
- sort=username:asc,realname:desc
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
RSQL filter to limit results. Supports all user fields.

Examples:
- filter=username=="john*"
- filter=realname=="John Smith"
- filter=email=="*@jamf.com"
- filter=position=="Manager";id!="1"
- filter=id=in=(123,456,789)
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort is id:asc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with a comma. Example: sort=date:desc,name:asc

get.summary: Get Mobile Device objects

get.description: Return paginated Mobile Device Inventory records

parameters.description: section of mobile device details, if not specified, General section data is returned. Multiple section parameters are supported, e.g. section=GENERAL&section=HARDWARE
parameters.name: section

parameters.description:
Sorting criteria in the format: property:asc/desc. Default sort
is displayName:asc. Multiple sort criteria are supported and must be separated with
a comma. 

Fields allowed in the sort: `airPlayPassword`, `appAnalyticsEnabled`, `assetTag`, `availableSpaceMb`, 
`batteryLevel`, `batteryHealth`, `bluetoothLowEnergyCapable`, `bluetoothMacAddress`, `capacityMb`, 
`lostModeEnabledDate`, `declarativeDeviceManagementEnabled`, `deviceId`, `deviceLocatorServiceEnabled`,
`devicePhoneNumber`, `diagnosticAndUsageReportingEnabled`, `displayName`, `doNotDisturbEnabled`, 
`enrollmentSessionTokenValid`, `exchangeDeviceId`, `cloudBackupEnabled`, `osBuild`,
`osSupplementalBuildVersion`, `osVersion`, `osRapidSecurityResponse`, `ipAddress`, `itunesStoreAccountActive`,
`mobileDeviceId`, `managementId`, `languages`, `lastBackupDate`, `lastEnrolledDate`, `lastCloudBackupDate`,
`lastInventoryUpdateDate`, `locales`, `locationServicesForSelfServiceMobileEnabled`, `lostModeEnabled`,
`managed`, `mdmProfileExpirationDate`, `model`, `modelIdentifier`,
`modelNumber`, `modemFirmwareVersion`, `preferredVoiceNumber`, `quotaSize`, `residentUsers`,
`serialNumber`, `sharedIpad`, `supervised`, `tethered`,
`timeZone`, `udid`, `usedSpacePercentage`, `wifiMacAddress`,
`deviceOwnershipType`, `building`, `department`, `emailAddress`,
`fullName`, `userPhoneNumber`, `position`, `room`,
`username`, `appleCareId`, `leaseExpirationDate`,`lifeExpectancyYears`,
`poDate`, `poNumber`, `purchasePrice`, `purchasedOrLeased`,
`purchasingAccount`, `purchasingContact`, `vendor`, `warrantyExpirationDate`,
`activationLockEnabled`, `blockEncryptionCapable`, `dataProtection`, `fileEncryptionCapable`,
`hardwareEncryptionSupported`, `jailbreakStatus`, `passcodeCompliant`, `passcodeCompliantWithProfile`,
`passcodeLockGracePeriodEnforcedSeconds`, `passcodePresent`, `carrierSettingsVersion`,
`cellularTechnology`, `currentCarrierNetwork`, `currentMobileCountryCode`, `currentMobileNetworkCode`, 
`dataRoamingEnabled`, `eid`, `network`, `homeMobileCountryCode`, 
`homeMobileNetworkCode`, `iccid`, `imei`, `imei2`,
`meid`, `personalHotspotEnabled`, `voiceRoamingEnabled`, `roaming`, `lastLoggedInUsernameSelfService`, `lastLoggedInUsernameSelfServiceTimestamp`

Example: `sort=displayName:desc,username:asc`
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description:
Query in the RSQL format, allowing to filter mobile device collection.
Default filter is empty query - returning all results for the requested page.

Fields allowed in the query: `airPlayPassword`, `appAnalyticsEnabled`, `assetTag`, `availableSpaceMb`, 
`batteryLevel`, `bluetoothLowEnergyCapable`, `bluetoothMacAddress`, `capacityMb`, 
`declarativeDeviceManagementEnabled`, `deviceId`, `deviceLocatorServiceEnabled`, `devicePhoneNumber`,
`diagnosticAndUsageReportingEnabled`, `displayName`, `doNotDisturbEnabled`, `exchangeDeviceId`, 
`cloudBackupEnabled`, `osBuild`, `osSupplementalBuildVersion`, `osVersion`, `osRapidSecurityResponse`,
`ipAddress`, `itunesStoreAccountActive`, `mobileDeviceId`, `managementId`, `languages`, `lastInventoryUpdateDate`, `locales`,
`locationServicesForSelfServiceMobileEnabled`, `lostModeEnabled`, `managed`, `model`, 
`modelIdentifier`, `modelNumber`, `modemFirmwareVersion`, `preferredVoiceNumber`, `quotaSize`, 
`residentUsers`, `serialNumber`, `sharedIpad`, `supervised`,
`tethered`, `timeZone`, `udid`, `usedSpacePercentage`, 
`wifiMacAddress`, `building`, `department`, `emailAddress`,
`fullName`, `userPhoneNumber`, `position`, `room`,
`username`, `appleCareId`, `lifeExpectancyYears`, `poNumber`, 
`purchasePrice`, `purchasedOrLeased`, `purchasingAccount`, `purchasingContact`,
`vendor`, `activationLockEnabled`, `blockEncryptionCapable`, `dataProtection`, 
`fileEncryptionCapable`, `passcodeCompliant`, `passcodeCompliantWithProfile`, `passcodeLockGracePeriodEnforcedSeconds`,
`passcodePresent`, `carrierSettingsVersion`, `currentCarrierNetwork`,
`currentMobileCountryCode`, `currentMobileNetworkCode`, `dataRoamingEnabled`, `eid`,
`network`, `homeMobileCountryCode`, `homeMobileNetworkCode`, `iccid`,
`imei`, `imei2`, `meid`, `personalHotspotEnabled`,
`roaming`, `lastLoggedInUsernameSelfService`, `lastLoggedInUsernameSelfServiceTimestamp`,
`groupId`, `groupName`

This param can be combined with paging and sorting.
Example: `filter=displayName=="iPad"`
parameters.name: filter
